title: draba hederifolia subsp. cossonii
intro: Draba hederifolia subsp. cossonii, also known as the Alpine Whitlow Grass, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Draba hederifolia subsp. cossonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and protect against infection. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, and topical applications.
